_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: RM9 Billion for Ships That Do Not Exist
context: The PAC's eighth report on the Littoral Combat Ship programme confirmed RM6.08B spent with zero vessels delivered, while Boustead Naval Shipyard's accounts remain qualified.

cards.text: Six billion spent. Zero ships delivered. The contract continues.
cards.sub: LCS programme: started 2011, first delivery due 2019, none delivered by 2026.

cards.h: PAC found RM1.4B in payments without matching work milestones.
cards.s: Progress payments were disbursed based on timelines, not completion. MACC investigation: ongoing since 2022.
cards.d: PAC found RM1.4B in payments without matching work milestones. Progress payments were disbursed based on timelines, not completion. MACC investigation: ongoing since 2022.

cards.h: Boustead Naval Shipyard: government-linked, politically connected, not technically qualified.
cards.s: Original technical partner DCNS withdrew. Replacement arrangements remain unclear.
cards.d: Boustead Naval Shipyard: government-linked, politically connected, not technically qualified. Original technical partner DCNS withdrew. Replacement arrangements remain unclear.

cards.h: Malaysia's maritime patrol gap is now a national security risk.
cards.s: South China Sea incursions up 340% since 2019. The vessels meant to respond do not exist.
cards.d: Malaysia's maritime patrol gap is now a national security risk. South China Sea incursions up 340% since 2019. The vessels meant to respond do not exist.

cards.text: This is not a procurement scandal. It is a defence capability vacuum.
cards.sub: While Parliament debates accountability, the ocean is unpatrolled.

cards.text: Like paying a builder RM9 billion for a house, getting an empty lot, and then asking the same builder to explain what happened
cards.sub: The entity that failed to deliver is the same entity investigating the failure

cards.text: RM9B bought neither ships nor accountability. The system that approved, funded, and failed to deliver remains structurally unchanged.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
